mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) above 2% signals rising inflation expectations, leading to higher interest rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 5-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a tightening labor market, leading to higher wages and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) above 2% signals rising economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 20-day forward returns in high-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the Housing Starts (HOUST) below 1.2 million signals a weakening housing market, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-housing-starts periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4% signals a tightening monetary cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 5-day forward returns in high-interest-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the Nonfarm Payrolls (PAYEMS) above 200,000 signals a strengthening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-payrolls periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the Retail Sales (RSAFS) above 5% signals rising consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-retail-sales periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
